description_long: This sunshade features a central pole with eight evenly spaced, slender arms radiating outward in a symmetrical, starburst pattern. At the center, a circular hub connects the arms, and a small, round, blue-and-white striped canopy is attached to one side, providing limited shade. The arms appear to be made of metal, giving the structure a lightweight yet sturdy appearance. The canopy is relatively small compared to the span of the arms, suggesting it is designed for compact, targeted shading rather than broad coverage. The overall design is minimalist and functional, with no visible decorative elements along the edges. This type of sunshade is suitable for urban parks, outdoor seating areas, or sidewalk cafes where space is limited.
description: A minimalist sunshade with a central pole, eight metal arms, and a small blue-and-white striped canopy. The design is sleek and functional, ideal for compact urban spaces.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the symmetrical, starburst arrangement of the eight arms and the small circular canopy attached to one side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the arms and the round canopy, with the arms fanning out in a flat plane.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view mirroring the top-down, with the arms radiating outward and the canopy visible on the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thinness of the structure and the canopy's position.
attribute_support_structure_style: eight straight, evenly spaced metal arms radiating from a central pole in a starburst configuration
